lines.en_lit: And let me derive the Merciful One states: which is for himself” brings from his own

As it was taught “Which is for himself” he brings from his own and not from of the community. might not bring from of the community the community does not gain atonement through it, but bring from of his fellow priests, as his fellow priests do gain atonement through it. the verse states: “Which is for himself,”
might not bring but he did bring valid. the verse states again: which is for himself” the text repeats this is indispensable

And according to your reasoning, if his fellow priests do not acquire in how does it atone for them? Rather, the property [bei gazza] of Aaron is different that the Merciful One rendered it ownerless with regard to his fellow priests. here too, with regard to substitution, the property of Aaron is different that the Merciful One rendered it ownerless with regard to his fellow priests.

MISHNA: would walk through the Sanctuary until he reaches between the two curtains that separated the Sanctuary and the Holy of Holies, and between them cubit. Rabbi Yosei says: There was only one curtain there, as it is stated: “And the curtain shall divide for you between the Sanctuary and the Holy of Holies”

GEMARA: Rabbi Yosei is saying well to the Rabbis, And the Rabbis say to you: This applies in the Tabernacle, However, in the Second Temple, since there was no one-cubit partition teraksin] as it in the First Temple that there was and the Rabbis were uncertain with regard to the sanctity whether of the inside or of the outside prepared two curtains

The Sages taught: he walked between the altar and the candelabrum. the statement of Rabbi Yehuda. Rabbi Meir says between the table and the altar. And some say between the table and the wall. Whose Some say? Rav Ḥisda said: It is Rabbi Yosei who said entrance was positioned in the north.

And Rabbi Yehuda he said to you entrance was positioned in the south. And Rabbi Meir, in accordance with whose does he hold? If he holds in accordance with Rabbi Yehuda enter as Rabbi Yehuda; if he holds in accordance with Rabbi Yosei, he enter as Rabbi Yosei.
Actually, holds in accordance with Rabbi Yosei, and he said to you: The tables, were arranged north to south, and the table blocked him and could not enter
And if you wish, say Actually the were arranged east to west, and due to the Divine Presence, it was not proper conduct
